mechanism_narrative: A persistent decline in the federal funds rate reduces the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, directly boosting equity valuations over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Forward 20-day returns show no statistical difference between falling and rising rate periods (t-test p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate crosses above 7.0 percent, labor market deterioration triggers expectations of fiscal stimulus and Fed intervention, generating positive equity returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are negative or flat when UNRATE exceeds 7.0 percent for three consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: The joint contraction of industrial production and housing starts signals a severe demand shock that compresses corporate profit margins, depressing equity valuations over the following month.
failure_condition: Rank IC between the composite demand shock index and 20-day SPX returns remains above 0.05 over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of tight capacity utilization, a decline in industrial production forecasts a supply-driven earnings recession, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns following INDPRO declines are positive in more than 55 percent of observations during late-cycle expansions.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly contraction in real GDP growth below 1.5 percent signals an economic slowdown that reduces equity risk premiums, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month as investors price in policy support.
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 60 percent of quarters where GDPC1 growth drops below 1.5 percent.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous rises in inflation expectations (CPI) and tight monetary policy (FEDFUNDS) compress real yields and trigger equity repricing, producing negative forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Composite inflation-rate interaction index shows zero correlation with 20-day SPX returns across multiple market cycles.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in total nonfarm payrolls reflects improving corporate hiring demand, which leads positive S&P 500 returns over the following week as earnings visibility improves.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns following consecutive months of PAYEMS growth show no significant positive drift (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: In a low-interest-rate environment, a sharp decline in retail sales forecasts a consumption-driven earnings miss, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where RSAFS declines exceed 2 percent month-over-month.
